description: Centralized tool registry — single source of truth for tool enforcement and dt configuration.

docker_redirects.message: Use 'st docker' for the managed compose stack and named test environments. Commands: st docker status, st docker up, st docker down, st docker logs <service>, st docker restart <service>, st docker build, st docker backup/restore, st docker env-create <name>, st docker env-exec <name> <service> <cmd...>, st docker metrics.

service_redirects.message: Use rebuild.sh <project> for service management or worktree-services.sh start/stop <task-id> for worktree services. Never manage services via systemctl directly.
Never kill service processes directly. Use rebuild.sh <project> or worktree-services.sh stop <task-id> for worktree services.
Never start app servers directly. Use rebuild.sh <project> or worktree-services.sh start <task-id> for worktree services.
Never start app servers directly. Use rebuild.sh <project> or worktree-services.sh start <task-id> for worktree services.
Never start dev servers directly. Use rebuild.sh <project> or worktree-services.sh start <task-id> for worktree services.
Never start dev servers directly. Use rebuild.sh <project> or worktree-services.sh start <task-id> for worktree services.

Use 'db' CLI instead of raw psql. Commands: db tables [--counts], db schema <table>, db sample <table> [n], db query "SELECT...", db exec "UPDATE/DELETE...", db ddl "CREATE INDEX...", db count <table>. Use db -P <project> to target a specific project (summitflow, agent-hub, portfolio-ai, hatchet).

Use 'st cleanup path <literal-path> [<literal-path>...] --recursive' for repo-local file or directory deletions. For task branch/worktree cleanup, use 'st cleanup worktrees --auto'. 'st cleanup path' validates repo root, blocks protected directories (.git, .st, node_modules), and supports --dry-run.
